mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for the return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive return frequency above 58%, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of industrial production contraction during a high-inflation environment signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% annualized and INDPRO contracts, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day return frequency exceeds 60% when CPIAUCSL growth surpasses the 3.5% threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth shows no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining retail sales indicates a synchronized demand collapse that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where both GDP growth and retail sales decline simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to beat a baseline benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal expanding manufacturing output and higher near-term corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with industrial production changes, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during expansion months.
